name: Sourdough Nepalese Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In the sourdough starter, beat the egg and add the 1/2 cup milk.
2. Sift together all dry ingredients; gradually add to the starter mixture.
3. Work until a smooth ball forms. Add more flour if needed. The dough should be soft, not sticky.
4. Cover and let rise for about 30 minutes.
5. Divide into 4 equal pieces. Shape each into a ball.
6. On a lightly floured board, roll out each ball into an 8-inch circle.
7. Cut into quarters.
8. Heat 1 1/2 inches of oil in a heavy skillet to 400F (200C).
9. Lower one portion at a time into the hot oil using tongs or slotted spatula.
10. As soon as it floats to the surface, turn with tongs to cook on the other side for 2 minutes, until puffed and golden brown.
11. Lift out with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
12. Serve warm. Good with lamb stew.
To make the starter sponge the night before, mix 1 1/2 cups water, 1 1/2 cups flour, and your sourdough starter in a bowl. Add 1 tablespoon sugar. Cover and let set overnight at room temperature. The next day, use as needed and return any remaining reserve to the refrigerator with a dash of sugar for later use.
